template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Which technique and malware are used by both intrusion sets {is1:intrusion-set.name} (ID {is1.id}) and {is2:intrusion-set.name} (ID {is2.id})? Recommend a course of action for that technique.

Answer: An attack pattern used by both intrusion sets {is1.name} and {is2.name} is {ap1:is1.uses>attack-pattern.name} (ID {ap1.id}). Both intrusion sets rely on malware {m1:is1.uses>malware.name} (ID {m1.id}). A course of action recommended for that technique is {coa1:ap1.mitigates<.name}.  <* this is an 'invisible' constraint: {ap2:is2.uses>attack-pattern.id} {force  ap1.id=ap2.id}  {m2:is2.uses>malware.id} {force m1.id=m2.id} {force m1.uses.id=ap1.id} {ap1.id[_="T1027.013"]} *>
template_object.schema: course-of-action - mitigates - attack-pattern, intrusion-set - uses - malware, malware - uses - attack-pattern

query: MATCH (is1:`intrusion-set`), (is2:`intrusion-set`), (is1:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(ap1:`attack-pattern`), (ap1:`attack-pattern`), (is1:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(m1:malware), (m1:malware), (ap1:`attack-pattern`)<-[:mitigates]-(coa1:`course-of-action`), (is2:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(ap2:`attack-pattern`), (ap2:`attack-pattern`), (is2:`intrusion-set`)-[:uses]->(m2:malware), (m2:malware), (m1:malware)-[:uses]->(_attack_pattern1:`attack-pattern`)     WHERE ap1.mitre_id = ap2.mitre_id AND m1.mitre_id = m2.mitre_id AND _attack_pattern1.mitre_id = ap1.mitre_id AND elementId(is1) <> elementId(is2) AND (ap1.mitre_id="T1027.013")     LIMIT 2000     RETURN DISTINCT is1.name, is1.mitre_id, is2.name, is2.mitre_id, ap1.name, ap1.mitre_id, m1.name, m1.mitre_id, coa1.name, ap2.mitre_id, m2.mitre_id, _attack_pattern1.mitre_id     ORDER BY rand()
generated_strings:
Instruction: You are a cybersecurity expert that has been trained to give precise responses to complex cybersecurity questions. You work in a SOC protecting data for enterprise customers helping to protect their digital assets.

Question: Which technique and malware are used by both intrusion sets Transparent Tribe (ID G0134) and TA2541 (ID G1018)? Recommend a course of action for that technique.

Answer: An attack pattern used by both intrusion sets Transparent Tribe and TA2541 is Encrypted/Encoded File (ID T1027.013). Both intrusion sets rely on malware njRAT (ID S0385). A course of action recommended for that technique is Behavior Prevention on Endpoint.
